# PRD — Product Requirements Document (Execution-oriented)

## 0) Document Control
- Version:
- Date:
- PM/Owner:
- Status: Draft | Review | Approved
- Linked SRS version:

## 1) Problem Statement
- User pain
- Current gap
- Why now

## 2) Product Goals & Success Metrics
- Goal 1 / Metric / Target
- Goal 2 / Metric / Target
- Guardrail metrics

## 3) User Segments & Jobs-to-be-Done
- Segment A
- Segment B
- JTBD mapping

## 4) Scope Definition
### 4.1 In Scope (MVP)
- Feature list with IDs (PRD-F-001...)

### 4.2 Out of Scope
- Explicitly excluded features

## 5) Feature Requirements
For each feature:
- Feature ID
- User value
- Functional behavior
- Edge cases
- Acceptance criteria
- Dependencies
- Risk level

## 6) UX Requirements
- Key screens
- UX principles
- Critical interactions
- Accessibility and localization requirements

## 7) Technical Strategy (high level)
- Architecture summary
- API/data touchpoints
- Migration/compatibility notes

## 8) Rollout Plan
- Milestones
- Phases
- Go/No-Go criteria

## 9) Telemetry & Analytics
- Events
- KPIs dashboards
- Alert thresholds

## 10) QA / Validation Plan
- Test levels
- UAT scenarios
- Sign-off checklist

## 11) Operations & Support
- Runbook pointers
- Incident handling expectations

## 12) Risks / Trade-offs / Decisions
- Decision log
- Known trade-offs

## 13) Open Questions
- Owner + due date for each

---

## Quick Quality Checklist
- [ ] Every feature maps to at least one business goal
- [ ] MVP scope is explicit and small
- [ ] Rollout criteria are measurable
- [ ] Dependencies and risks are explicit
- [ ] Acceptance criteria are testable
